data.title: FAA’s planned $3.1M fine on Boeing is too lenient, senator says
data.description:
Senator Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.) is pushing back against the Federal Aviation Administration’s plan to fine Boeing (NYSE:BA) $3.1 million for safety lapses, arguing the penalty is far too small to drive change.

“For Boeing, such fines are easily absorbed as the cost of doing business, not a meaningful deterrent to dangerous behavior,” Blumenthal wrote in a letter to FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford. “Unless penalties rise to the level that forces the company to invest in real safety reforms, the risks to the flying public will persist.”

The FAA said it identified hundreds of violations in Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) quality-control systems at the company’s 737 assembly line in Renton, Washington, as well as at Spirit AeroSystems’ (NYSE:SPR) fuselage plant in Wichita, Kansas.

data.title: flyExclusive joins Russell U.S. Indexes
data.description: • flyExclusive (NYSE:FLYX) announced that it has been added to the Russell 2000, Russell 3000 and Russell Microcap Indexes, as part of the quarterly initial public offering update, effective on Monday, September 22.

data.title: Cintas shares little changed on quarterly results, forward earnings guidance
data.description:
Cintas (NASDAQ:CTAS) shares were little changed on Wednesday after the company reported fiscal first-quarter 2026 results that topped Wall Street estimates but delivered only a modest guidance raise. Its shares have slumped about 12% from a peak in early June.

The uniform and business services provider posted revenue of $2.72 billion for the quarter ended Aug. 31, up 8.7% from a year earlier and in line with the $2.7 billion analysts anticipated. Acquisitions contributed 0.9 percentage points to the increase, while organic growth was 7.8%.

Net income rose 8.7% year over year to $491.1 million, with earnings per share climbing 9.1% to $1.20, matching the consensus estimate. Operating income rose 10% to $617.9 million, with margins improving 30 basis points to 22.7%.

The company’s effective tax rate climbed to 17.6% from 15.8% a year earlier, and management guided to a 20% tax rate for the full year. Net interest expense is also expected to rise to $97 million from $95 million a year earlier.

Cintas (NASDAQ:CTAS) also returned capital to shareholders, repurchasing $347 million in stock during the quarter and paying out a quarterly dividend of $182.3 million, up 15% from the prior year.

Chief Executive Todd Schneider in a statement credited “disciplined execution, ongoing investment in technology and talent, and the unwavering commitment of our employee-partners” for the strong quarter, adding that Cintas remains positioned for “sustainable growth and long-term value creation.”

Looking ahead, the company raised its fiscal 2026 revenue outlook to a range of $11.06 billion to $11.18 billion, compared with a prior range of $11.00 billion to $11.15 billion. Earnings are now expected between $4.74 and $4.86 a share, up slightly from its earlier forecast of $4.71 to $4.85. Wall Street analysts, on average, project earnings of $4.85 a share and revenue of about $11.1 billion for the current fiscal year.

data.title: U.S. in talks for $20B swap line to Argentina, Bessent says
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the White House is in discussions for a $20 billion swap line with the country's central bank and was ready to buy its dollar debt, extending support to Argentine President Javier Milei.

"The U.S. Treasury stands ready to purchase Argentina’s USD bonds and will do so as conditions warrant. We are also prepared to deliver significant standby credit via the Exchange Stabilization Fund, and we have been in active discussions with President Milei’s team to do so," Bessent said in a post on X.

The country's benchmark, the S&P MERVAL Index (MERVAL) was up 0.24%. The Global X MSCI Argentina ETF (NYSEARCA:ARGT), which provides U.S. investors with exposure to Argentine equities, rose ~4%. 

Earlier in the month, Argentina's financial markets were facing significant turbulence, with domestic stocks and U.S.-listed ETFs seeing a downturn amid a strained political environment. The sell-off was driven by Milei's ruling party losing in a key provincial election in the Buenos Aires province.

"In addition, the United States stands ready to purchase secondary or primary government debt and we are working with the Argentine government to end the tax holiday for commodity producers converting foreign exchange," Bessent added. 

Argentina's economy has been battling challenges for the past few months. In April, the International Monetary Fund reached a preliminary agreement with Argentina on a $20 billion bailout.

Bessent also said that he has been in touch with many U.S. companies who are looking to make "substantial" foreign direct investments in Argentina in multiple sectors in the event of a positive election outcome.

"Argentina has the tools to defeat speculators, including those who seek to destabilize Argentina’s markets for political objectives," he said. 

Milei won praise from Trump and his allies for aggressively slashing government spending, but his popularity is waning at home amid economic contraction and rising unemployment.

Other Argentina-related stocks: BBVA Argentina (NYSE:BBAR) +9.8%, Banco Macro (NYSE:BMA) +9.7%, Grupo Financiero Galicia (NASDAQ:GGAL) +10.3%, and Grupo Supervielle (NYSE:SUPV) +8.8%.

data.title: California Resources Corporation announces Offering of $400M
data.description:
• California Resources Corporation (NYSE:CRC)  stated a private offering of $400M in senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by the company's existing subsidiaries that guarantee its revolving credit facility, its 7.125% senior unsecured notes due 2026 and its 8.250% senior unsecured notes due 2029. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to repay the existing indebtedness of Berry Corporation.

data.title: Encore Capital announces $400M senior secured notes
data.description:
•  Encore Capital Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:ECPG) announced private offering of $400M of senior secured notes due 2031.
  • The Company intends to use the proceeds from this offering to repay drawings under its revolving credit facility and to pay certain transaction fees.

data.title: Comstock to acquire multifamily property in Maryland
data.description:
Asset manager Comstock (NASDAQ:CHCI) announced on Wednesday a purchase agreement to acquire a 400+ unit multifamily property in the City of Rockville in Montgomery County, Maryland.

As part of the agreement, the company has a 45-day due diligence period.

Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter.

"This property is consistent with our strategic growth plan, is a natural fit for our managed portfolio, and is well-positioned to contribute to future revenue growth while delivering value for our strategic partners, our residents, and our shareholders," said CEO Christopher Clemente.

data.title: Stock market advances a day after Wall Street's rally takes a pause
data.description:
Wall Street edged up Wednesday, a day after Wall Street’s three-day record-setting streak stalled.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2% and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.14%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.59%.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) edged lower in early trade, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

"Powell’s Rhode Island excursion was of more market interest. Powell deliberately noted that both inflation and unemployment are moving in the wrong direction from the Fed’s perspective," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

"Markets struggled to keep up with the recent momentum yesterday, with the S&P 500 finally slipping back after a succession of record highs, with a couple of main drivers for this retreat. One was a reversal in tech, as there were as many questions as answers over Nvidia's high-profile $100B tie-up with OpenAI from Monday. The other was a slightly more cautious tone on the labor market by Powell," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

But the bigger concern from an AI bubble risk perspective was whether Nvidia was now relying on investments into customers to feed its revenue growth and whether we’re beginning to see some creative accounting reminiscent of 1990s telco deals, Reid added. 

On the day ahead now, the August New Home Sales report will land during market hours. The consensus looks for a retreat to 649K in the month. 

San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will speak on the economic outlook before the annual Spencer Fox Eccles Convocation.
